commands.review_summary:
The PR adds active-memory recall prompt caveats for mutable operational facts and changes summary truncation to append an ellipsis, with targeted tests updated and added.

PR surface: Source +7, Tests +17. Total +24 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection gives a high-confidence reproduction path: current main's active-memory prompt lacks stale operational fact caveats, and current truncateSummary clips text without an incompleteness marker. I did not run a live recall scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The next action is contributor/maintainer validation: the code path is focused, but the external PR needs real behavior proof and PR-body evidence before merge.

target.title: fix(active-memory): caveat mutable ops facts; mark truncated recall as incomplete
target.body:
Recall guardrails so stale operational state isn't presented as current truth (FAD-970).

- Mutable operational facts (cron/job health, automation status, deployments, service availability) recalled by active-memory now carry freshness/staleness caveats and are not summarized as "running/healthy" unless the memory itself has current evidence; the answering model is told to verify live.
- `truncateSummary()` appends an ellipsis when clipping, so truncated recall reads as visibly incomplete instead of authoritative.
- Tests: active-memory shard 153 passing (3 targeted).

Authored by the costaff agent; opened on its behalf.
target.author: spencer2211
